title: quercus × beckyae
intro: Quercus × beckyae, a hybrid of the Quercus coccinea and Quercus rubra, is a deciduous tree species commonly known as Becky's oak or red oak hybrid. The bark and leaves of the Quercus × beckyae plant are used in herbal medicine, and it is valued for its tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Quercus × beckyae include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be dried and used as a supplement.
